user_query:
I'm the owner of a 2-tech residential plumbing company in Austin. Please read the following files for context and task list:
- input/company_profile.json (location, team size, base hourly rate, target margin, after-hours multiplier, and notes)
- input/common_tasks.csv (rows of common residential service tasks with category, base labor hours, and a materials SKU list column)
- input/materials_catalog.csv (SKU, description, unit_cost for materials)

Deliverables (write exactly to these paths):
1) output/pricing/flat_rate_book.csv — Build a flat-rate pricing book for at least 25 common residential tasks from input/common_tasks.csv. Use the base hourly labor rate in input/company_profile.json or default to $140/hr if missing. Compute material_cost by summing unit_cost for SKUs listed per task in input/materials_catalog.csv (assume quantity 1 each unless the task row specifies quantities). Calculate labor_cost = labor_hours × hourly_rate. Set flat_rate_price to achieve a 55%–65% gross margin on each task. Include after_hours_price using a 1.5×–2× multiplier (use company_profile.json if provided, otherwise apply 1.7×). Required columns in this order: task, category, labor_hours, material_cost, labor_cost, flat_rate_price, margin_pct, after_hours_price. Make sure at least one drain cleaning task (priced $150–$350) and water heater installs (both tank and tankless, within industry ranges) are included.
2) output/pricing/assumptions.md — A concise note (<= 1 page) explaining your margin math, how you handled SKUs/quantities, the after-hours policy, and any permitting/compliance considerations for water heater replacements in residential work.
3) output/ops/dispatch_sop.md — A 1-page dispatching SOP tailored to a 2-tech shop using zone-based scheduling, 2-hour service windows (8–10, 10–12, 12–2, 2–4), a 10-minute morning huddle, reserved callback slots, and tech utilization targets. Include guidance on drive-time limits and priority order.
4) output/inventory/truck_stock.csv — A truck stock and par-level list (>= 15 lines) covering at least: fittings, valves, drain supplies, water heater parts, fixtures, and tools. Columns: item, category, size, par_min, par_max, notes.
5) output/marketing/plan.json — A 90-day residential marketing plan that allocates a total budget of $12,000 across channels including Google LSA, Google Business Profile, SEO, and a referral program. For each channel include a numeric budget, target_cpl (cost per lead) within $25–$75 for residential, and target_booking_rate >= 0.75. Also include a reviews_goal for Google Business Profile (>= 50). Ensure channel budgets sum exactly to the total budget.

Please flag any risks or assumptions you made, and keep everything aligned with best practices for residential plumbing operations in our market.
